text.title: Stat-Power Pre-Check Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Stat-Power Pre-Check application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: All five inputs (weekly_visitors, baseline_conversion, target_relative_lift, alpha, power) are typed and present. Missing any = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Verdict math is only as good as the inputs; missing inputs make verdicts folklore.

rule.statement: No experiment launches without a recorded GO / STRETCH / KILL verdict in the ledger.
rule.rationale: Launching without a verdict is how underpowered tests proliferate.

rule.statement: Minimum 100 conversions per variant before reading results. Verdicts with smaller minimums are flagged as exploratory.
rule.rationale: Sub-100 conversions per variant produces noise indistinguishable from signal.

rule.statement: No sequential / 'peeking' stops without a documented sequential-testing method (e.g. SPRT, Bayesian).
rule.rationale: Naive peeking inflates false-positive rate dramatically.

rule.statement: Target lift is expressed as a relative percentage (e.g. 10%), never absolute percentage points. Absolute lifts are converted to relative before scoring.
rule.rationale: Relative lift normalises across pages with different baselines.

rule.statement: Test window is declared up front. Extending without a new verdict invalidates the test.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
